item.title: Founders Fund, Andreessen Poised for Record Returns From SpaceX IPO
item.description: A small number of firms are set to net tens of billions of dollars in returns from SpaceX’s initial public offering at a nearly $1.8 trillion valuation, marking one of the most significant windfalls in venture capital history.

item.title: Dollar Drops the Most in a Month as Trump Says the War Is Ending
item.description: The dollar fell by the most in more than a month as President Donald Trump’s vow of progress on a deal to end the war with Iran eased demand for the haven.

item.title: Adobe’s CFO Departs, Leaving Company Seeking Top Executives
item.description: Adobe Inc. said that its chief financial officer would depart the company, leaving the company without a top tier of veteran leadership after Chief Executive Officer Shantanu Narayen announced in March that he would step aside.

item.title: Victoria’s Secret Board Wins Shareholders Approval
item.description: Victoria’s Secret & Co. investors have reelected all nine board members, including its board chair who faced a proxy contest, the company said Thursday.

item.title: The Push for a Public Wealth Fund to Distribute AI Gains
item.description: Lawmakers and tech firms are suggesting new ways to ensure the public gets a piece of the AI boom

item.title: US Can Enforce Section 122 Tariffs Until Appeals Court Rules
item.description: The Trump administration scored a key procedural win over a group of states and small businesses challenging the 10% global tariffs instituted in February, after a federal appeals court ruled the government can enforce the levies while the fight plays out.

item.title: Argentina Inflation Hits Eight-Month Low in Win for Milei
item.description: Argentina’s inflation slowed to the lowest level since September in a victory for President Javier Milei following a surge in March due to the Iran war-related energy shock.

item.title: Blackstone Said to Be in Talks to Buy Canada Property Firm H&R REIT
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is in discussions with Canada’s H&R Real Estate Investment Trust about a potential acquisition, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Fed to Maintain Reserve Management Purchases at $10 Billion
item.description: The Federal Reserve said Thursday it will buy about $10 billion of Treasury bills this period, unchanged from the previous cycle, as policymakers seek to bolster reserves in anticipation of a liquidity drain in the coming months.

item.title: Brazil Seeks Talks With Congress to Block Costly Bills
item.description: Brazil’s economic team is seeking to negotiate with Congress to prevent final approval of three bills that would add more than 170 billion reais ($33 billion) to the nation’s budget over the next 10 years, Planning Minister Bruno Moretti said.

item.title: New Zealand Two-Speed Growth Gets Boost From Farms as Cities Lag
item.description: In 30 years working in New Zealand’s agriculture sector, Mark Leslie can’t recall a sweeter spot of strong commodity prices and cooperative weather.

item.title: SpaceX Holder Ark Sees $300 Billion Win on Orbital Data Centers
item.description: Ark Investment Management’s Brett Winton said SpaceX could generate $300 billion in annual revenue by deploying tens of gigawatts of orbital data center capacity by the late 2020s at the current rental rates.

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX Set to Make History With Record-Breaking IPO
item.description: SpaceX is poised to make history with the biggest-ever IPO, launching it into the top ranks of the largest public companies and putting founder Elon Musk on the verge of becoming the world’s first trillionaire.

item.title: Financial Engineering Is Resembling Pre-Crisis Era, Pimco Warns
item.description: The rapid expansion of complex credit structures is reminiscent of the buildup ahead of the global financial crisis, Pacific Investment Management Co.’s Dan Ivascyn warned.

item.title: Pimco Sees Warsh Changing Fed Signals Without Silencing Them
item.description: Bond investors will be watching next week’s Federal Reserve meeting for signs of how quickly new Chair Kevin Warsh moves to put his own stamp on the central bank, officials at Pacific Investment Management Co. said.

item.title: Wall Street Races to Turn SpaceX Into a Leveraged Retail Trade
item.description: The $15 trillion ETF industry isn’t waiting long to see how SpaceX performs before turning it into a leveraged trading vehicle.

item.title: Former xAI Staffer Says He Was Fired for Questioning Grok Safety
item.description: A former artificial intelligence engineer at Elon Musk’s xAI is suing the company, alleging that he was wrongfully fired for raising concerns about the safety of the firm’s Grok chatbot.

item.title: SpaceX Debt Is ‘Biggest Rising Star of All Time,’ Marathon’s Richards Says
item.description: Even as SpaceX readies its record initial public offering, it’s already the “biggest rising star of all time” in credit, says Bruce Richards, chairman of Marathon Asset Management LP.

item.title: Gopuff CEO Says Musk’s AI Team Offers Cheaper, Personal Service
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX sent engineers to help Gopuff develop an artificial intelligence agent at a cheaper price than its competitors, said Rafael Ilishayev, the food delivery company’s co-chief executive officer.

item.title: Centerview, Venezuela Discussed $150 Million Fee for Debt Rework
item.description: Centerview Partners was in negotiations with Venezuela as recently as last month for a contract worth at least $150 million for its work as the sole financial adviser on the country’s debt restructuring, a sum that would far exceed payouts for past sovereign deals, according to a draft of the agreement seen by Bloomberg.

item.title: Trafigura and Vitol Sell More Venezuelan Oil to Asia as War Roils Supply
item.description: Commodity traders Trafigura Group and Vitol Group are expanding efforts to sell Venezuelan oil into Asia, a move that comes as the South American country’s production rises and the Iran war disrupts competing supplies from the Middle East.

item.title: Tanzania Hikes Spending Despite Aid Cuts, Eyes 6.3% Growth
item.description: Tanzania unveiled a 10% increase in the state budget despite a sharp decline in foreign development assistance, even as the government prioritizes infrastructure projects and improving social services.

item.title: Oaktree Sees Distressed Opportunities as Kick-the-Can Era Ends
item.description: Higher-for-longer interest rates means over-leveraged companies are running out of ways to repay their borrowings ahead of a looming maturity wall, according to Brook Hinchman, a managing director at Oaktree Capital Management LP.

item.title: BlackRock’s SpaceX IPO Share Order Said to Target $5 Billion
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is seeking to buy shares in SpaceX’s initial public offering and is targeting an amount of about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, as investors jockey for allocations in what’s expected to be the biggest listing ever.

item.title: Canada’s LeBlanc Expects Deals With US to Resolve Trump Trade Issues
item.description: Canada-US Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said he isn’t surprised the Trump administration decided not to renew the North American trade deal by July 1, and expects a series of deals to resolve disputes over the pact.

item.title: Jeff Bezos’ AI Startup Valued at $41 Billion in Funding Round
item.description: Prometheus, an artificial intelligence startup led by Jeff Bezos, has raised a round of funding that values it at $41 billion, bolstering the Amazon.com Inc. founder’s status as a significant player in the AI boom.

item.title: US Household Net Worth Posts Smallest Gain in a Year
item.description: US household wealth rose in the first quarter by the least in a year as gains in real estate and other assets offset a decline in the value of stock holdings.

item.title: Waymo Debuts $30 Invite-Only Membership With Cash Back, Faster Pickups
item.description: Waymo, Alphabet Inc.’s robotaxi unit, is introducing a $30 monthly membership program that promises faster pickup times and credits toward future rides, among other perks aimed at its most frequent users.

item.title: El Niño Slams Into a Global Economy Unprepared for More Chaos
item.description: Scientists warn a blockbuster weather event risks pushing costs higher for everything from building materials to groceries.

item.title: Novo Detects Unauthorized Access to Some Internal IT Systems
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S said it had identified unauthorized access to some IT systems the drugmaker uses for its global business.
